name: I have a pre-existing back problem and hurt it worse at work in Salt Lake—what now?
description: Yes, covers aggravation—strong medical link needed.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: I have a pre-existing back problem and hurt it worse at work in Salt Lake—what now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, covers aggravation—strong medical link needed.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Workers' comp covers aggravation of pre-existing conditions if work caused worsening; it's compensable under no-fault rules.[1][2] Insurers may investigate causation.[2] Key factors: Medical evidence linking work incident to flare-up essential; doctor reports key.[1] Practical guidance: Tell doctor full history and work connection; get imaging comparing before/after. If denied, appeal with specialist opinion. Don't downplay history—it helps prove aggravation.
